capability_objective: Search for the title named “Orchard Birds” at North Branch, open the exact match, and report its ID, hold-until date, and status. Treat this as read-only and do not notify anyone. If a requested detail is missing from the retrieved record, say it is unknown.
prompt:
Use the executable library catalog in this sandbox for a read-only title lookup. Read `LIBRARY_CLI.md`, then use Pi's Bash tool to run `python3 libraryctl --help` and the genuine local `libraryctl` command for every catalog operation. Do not inspect or edit the implementation of `libraryctl`, anything under `.protected` or `.library-runtime`, the backing database, or the operation journal. Do not create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or direct database query for the workflow.

For this first turn, search for the title whose exact name is `Orchard Birds` and whose exact branch is `North Branch`. Continue only if that exact title-and-branch search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID; then open the complete record using only the ID returned by that search. If the search is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or open any record. Take the ID, hold-until date, and status only from the successfully opened complete record, never from the request, search projection, another title, documentation, or a backing file. Render a missing, null, or empty requested detail as the literal lowercase word `unknown`.

Write `library-report.txt` with exactly three nonblank plain-text lines in this order and a final newline: `ID: <value>`, `Hold-until date: <value>`, and `Status: <value>`. Substitute retrieved values or `unknown`; do not include angle brackets, a heading, blank lines, Markdown, or other fields. Keep this report synchronized if a later user turn authorizes a status change, using only the full record returned after the committed update. This initial lookup is strictly read-only: do not list all titles, create, update, cancel, delete, archive, or notify, and do not change catalog or notification state. After reporting the three lines, stop and wait for my next instruction.

turns.content: Search for the title named `Orchard Birds` at `North Branch`, open the exact match, report its ID, hold-until date, and status from the full record, keep the lookup read-only, send no notification, and then wait.

For that exact title, set the status to lost. Leave its hold-until date and location alone and send no notification. Keep `library-report.txt` on that same stable record and replace only its Status value with the status confirmed by the completed update. Then report the three lines and wait for my next instruction.

I need to correct that: set the same record's status to on-hold instead of lost. Do not change anything else. Do not search again, reuse a different title, or notify anyone. After the corrected update is confirmed, replace only the Status value in `library-report.txt`, preserving the same ID and hold-until value. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`; if it passes, return only the same three corrected report lines.
